scenario_name: Translation and localization assistant for confidential business content
description: An enterprise deploys an LLM-backed translation/localization assistant accessed by internal teams. The assistant handles strategy documents, contracts, internal communications, and sometimes regulated content. Translation reveals the source content to the model and to logs; cross-lingual safety-filter weakness (well-documented in the jailbreak literature) creates a side-channel where translated outputs may include content the original-language filter would have blocked.
assumptions: Hosted LLM under enterprise data-isolation contract.
Translation requests routed through a corporate gateway with DLP.
Specific regulated content (e.g. clinical trial protocols, MNPI) is excluded by policy.

components.description: DLP at gateway + cross-lingual content classifier.
Corporate IDP issuing per-user tokens with team attribute.
Prompt + output audit log with retention aligned to record management.

risks_identified.rationale: Source content frequently confidential (strategy, contracts, internal comms); the assistant sees it in its entirety.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: DLP at gateway blocks marked-confidential content; provider no-training contract.

risks_identified.rationale: Users may use unsanctioned consumer translators for convenience, exfiltrating content.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Org-wide AI-use policy; endpoint DLP partial.

risks_identified.rationale: Cross-lingual safety-filter weakness: translation may render content that the source-language filter would have blocked.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Output safety classifier in target language enabled; coverage uneven for low-resource languages.

risks_identified.rationale: Audit logs hold full source and translated text including sensitive content.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Logs encrypted; access role-gated; retention aligned to records policy.

risks_identified.rationale: GDPR / data-residency obligations for the source content; some jurisdictions require translation services to comply with sectoral rules.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Provider terms include residency for EU users; sectoral exclusions enforced by policy.

existing_controls.description: DLP at gateway scans source content for confidential markers; blocks marked content.
Output safety classifier in target language on translations.
Org AI-use policy; endpoint DLP on managed laptops.
Audit log access role-gated; retention aligned to records-management policy.
Sectoral content exclusions enforced by policy (MNPI, clinical, classified).

control_gaps.description: Cross-lingual safety classifier coverage uneven for low-resource languages.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Expand classifier coverage; route low-resource-language requests to a stricter review path.

control_gaps.description: Endpoint DLP for personal-device translation use is incomplete.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Enforce managed-device-only access via IDP conditional access.

control_gaps.description: Audit-log retention does not differentiate translated PII from raw.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Apply field-level redaction to translated outputs; separate retention bucket for sensitive translations.

framework_alignments.control_name: Approaches for mapping legal and AI risks
